signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Trade Optimism
signals.text: NZD outperformance (+0.710% avg) against CAD weakness (-0.920% avg) creates a 1.631% G8 spread, further supported by forecast-beating China trade data which bolsters the NZD proxy bid. The for USD/CAD is offset by CAD's status as the G8 laggard, while NZD COT net short -19.2k sets up a short-covering squeeze if the China-driven risk-on sentiment persists. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 10 Jun is the catalyst for assessing if CAD can recover from its current trend.
evidence: China April trade figures beat forecasts

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind Risk
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +58.9k (78.3% long) creates a significant crowded-long unwind risk despite the pair closing Friday at 0.725. While the -60bp carry disadvantage against USD and retail sentiment at 88% short suggest a contrarian bid, the heavy spec positioning leaves the pair vulnerable to any disappointment in upcoming US-China trade talks. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 16 Jun determines if the current hiking bias can sustain these overextended long positions.

signals.title: GBP/JPY — Speculative Long Overhang + Carry Demand
signals.text: GBP COT net long +31.2k (65.3% long) reinforces the upside bias in GBP/JPY following the Friday close above 213.00. The 300bp carry advantage for GBP over JPY continues to drive demand despite JPY's 1W momentum, while JPY COT net short -61.3k highlights a persistent spec bias against the Yen. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) on 16 Jun is the catalyst for determining if a hawkish shift can trigger a JPY short-squeeze.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Speculative Short Pressure
signals.text: EUR COT net short -21.0k maintains downside pressure on EUR/USD as the pair faces a 175bp carry disadvantage against the Fed. While the pair gained +0.52% last week to close at 1.179, the heavy spec shorting and neutral ECB bias limit the scope for a sustained breakout. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Jun serves as the catalyst for any shift in the current hold regime.
